title: Odontotrichum napellifolium
intro: Odontotrichum napellifolium, also known as Tibetan bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Tibetan medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Odontotrichum napellifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
